title: Gentlemen Its a Nuclear Device
wikitext:
[]: "Gentlemen, It's a Nuclear Device"             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Apr 17, 2013 6:40 pm As I was going through episode clips recently, the following image from episode #24 caught my eye. Notice the red heptagons with nuclear symbols in the background as Kaworu ponders over Lilith (soon to be formerly known as Adam) [wkimg]24 C277 dc lilith.jpg[/wkimg] It's more than likely that these are (appropriately oversized) nuclear/N2 bombs, surrounding Lilith's chamber, to be used by Nerv as a last ditch measure in case an Angel (or Ikari) gains access to the chamber. In fact, their presence was alluded to earlier in this very episode. Episode #24  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Apr 17, 2013 6:44 pm The red heptagons don't have the nuclear symbols on them in 25' and 26'. What gives?

[]: Re: "Gentlemen, It's a Nuclear Device"             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Apr 17, 2013 6:57 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I believe that the significance the bombs' presence in the shot is confirmation that we are really seeing the inside of Terminal Dogma here. This isn't a dreamscape or mental contruct. Gendo's torso and glasses are really here, and his lower half is really untanged. Again, I believe the evidence is pointing us towards an exceptional fate for Gendo in EoE, and that something has literally -- not allegorically -- occurred here.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 17, 2013 7:41 pm The Gendo discussion should probably go here .

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Wed Apr 17, 2013 8:03 pm This is going to sound weird, but check out the part of episode 24 when the defeated Unit-02 goes crashing through the wall, and then the victorious Unit-01 marches in and grabs Kaworu. See the wall panel that Unit-02 crashed trough? Yep, it's one of the heptagons! Does that mean the heptagons are just fancy wall panels? Strange. But I guess it could still be a futuristic bomb of some sort, in which case they're lucky it didn't go off when Unit-02 crashed through it. Yikes! Or maybe the panels are warning signs pointing to where the actual bombs are? At any rate, it's an interesting detail, and now I'm really curious what it meant.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Apr 18, 2013 3:56 am Literary Eagle wrote: This is going to sound weird, but check out the part of episode 24 when the defeated Unit-02 goes crashing through the wall, and then the victorious Unit-01 marches in and grabs Kaworu. See the wall panel that Unit-02 crashed trough? Yep, it's one of the heptagons! Does that mean the heptagons are just fancy wall panels? Strange. But I guess it could still be a futuristic bomb of some sort, in which case they're lucky it didn't go off when Unit-02 crashed through it. Yikes! Or maybe the panels are warning signs pointing to where the actual bombs are? At any rate, it's an interesting detail, and now I'm really curious what it meant.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Thu Apr 18, 2013 5:01 am So uh, did anyone else never notice Gendo's torso in that shot with the glasses before? Not sure about the bombs though. Perhaps they're meant to tie how Gendo treated everyone so coldly with the kind of coldness you'd need to drop an explosive on an area. His death is rather graphic and personal in comparison.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Apr 18, 2013 11:07 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: So uh, did anyone else never notice Gendo's torso in that shot with the glasses before?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 18, 2013 11:26 am No one seems to have noticed it so far, but what I find interesting about those heptagons is that they seem have rotated a bit from 24 to EoE. Gotta wonder what's up with that.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Thu Apr 18, 2013 12:42 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Don't you guys mean his legs? The torso is the part that's missing .

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Apr 18, 2013 4:54 pm I would agree with the original assessment that these heptagons are indeed Nuclear Weapons. Seems like the best way to obliterate anything inside of Nerv.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 18, 2013 5:02 pm Literary Eagle wrote: See the wall panel that Unit-02 crashed trough? Yep, it's one of the heptagons! Does that mean the heptagons are just fancy wall panels?

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Thu Apr 18, 2013 5:22 pm Bagheera wrote: No one seems to have noticed it so far, but what I find interesting about those heptagons is that they seem have rotated a bit from 24 to EoE. Gotta wonder what's up with that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 18, 2013 6:07 pm K40s wrote: It would be interesting to see a pic of Eva-02 crashing through the "nuclear wall" scene that was mentioned earlier.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 18, 2013 7:27 pm Down in Dogma, Heptagons stalk, hunting Hexagonal wasps.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Thu Apr 18, 2013 8:19 pm Thanks for the pictures, Reichu! Gosh, that really does look like a gigantic wasp nest. With Lilith as the baby-making queen, perhaps? Hmm, so hexagons on the "wasp nest", and heptagons in Lilith's chamber, and A.T. Fields are concentric octagons... wow, it's like watching an anime version of Square One .

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Apr 18, 2013 8:28 pm Literary Eagle wrote: Thanks for the pictures, Reichu! Gosh, that really does look like a gigantic wasp nest. With Lilith as the baby-making queen, perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Thu Apr 18, 2013 9:47 pm Thanks for the pics Reichu ! It doesn't quite seem to me that the nuclear heptagons and the who-knows-how-many-sides polygon that Eva-02 crashes through are the same, the "nuketagons" seem to be much smaller (or much farther away) maybe is the perspective that is [s]a bit[/s] quite messed up... the "crashtagon" seems to be in front of Lilith and much closer to her than the "nuketagons" on her left side, I'm not even sure if the "crashtagon" is a heptagon at all, it seems to have more than 7 sides, in those two pics you can count 4 sides and we don't get to see a full half of the "crashtagon", if I had to bet I'd say is, at the very least, an octagon that functions as the front door for the vault, similar to what we see in NME 1.11. Also isn't Lilith supposed to be in the centre of the vault? if the "crashtagon" is indeed one of the "nuketagons" (the same size and all) it would mean that Lilith is closer to that front wall than to the side wall we see in the first pic, that the vault is not a sphere (or any other symmetric shape) or that they simply messed up the perspective. Look at these two side by side to see what I mean: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Apr 19, 2013 1:30 pm K40s wrote: Also isn't Lilith supposed to be in the centre of the vault? if the "crashtagon" is indeed one of the "nuketagons" (the same size and all) it would mean that Lilith is closer to that front wall than to the side wall we see in the first pic, that the vault is not a sphere (or any other symmetric shape) or that they simply messed up the perspective. Look at these two side by side to see what I mean: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Apr 19, 2013 3:03 pm Some kind of simulated lens/optical effect is how I considered the heptagons in EoE as well. They're clearly out of focus as well. K40s wrote: It doesn't quite seem to me that the nuclear heptagons and the who-knows-how-many-sides polygon that Eva-02 crashes through are the same, the "nuketagons" seem to be much smaller (or much farther away).....
